flags.concern: Pinned-group sync suppression: the preferred boolean `isRestoringRef` alternative will not actually suppress the post-undo sync, because `restoreSnapshot()` would set and clear the ref before the `useEffect` triggered by `commitData(setData)` runs. The guard has to survive past the render boundary, not just the synchronous restore call.
flags.evidence: `useTimelineHistory.restoreSnapshot()` commits synchronously at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineHistory.ts:158-170`, and `commitData()` updates React state immediately at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts:156-203`. `usePinnedGroupSync()` only checks its skip condition inside the later timeout/effect path at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts:175-210,201-210`, so an immediately-reset boolean ref would already be false when the sync actually runs.

flags.concern: Pinned-group sync plumbing: step 3 says to wire the suppression ref through `useTimelineState.ts` "where both `useTimelineHistory` and `usePinnedGroupSync` are composed", but in this repo `usePinnedGroupSync` is mounted directly in `TimelineEditor.tsx`, not in `useTimelineState`. The file ownership is still incomplete unless the plan explicitly includes the `TimelineEditor` call site or another real path into that hook.
flags.evidence: `useTimelineState()` instantiates history at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts:65-68` and never calls `usePinnedGroupSync()`. The actual sync hook call is `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:592-599`.

flags.concern: Pinned-group sync verification: the plan's main regression assertion (`delete -> undo` does not trigger a follow-up sync mutation) cannot be proven in `useTimelineHistory.test.ts` or a pure `shot-group-commands.test.ts`, because those harnesses do not mount `usePinnedGroupSync` or expose an `applyEdit` spy. The existing sync tests live in `useClipEditing.test.ts`, and the planned validation run does not include that suite.
flags.evidence: `useTimelineHistory.test.ts` renders only `useTimelineHistory({ dataRef, commitData })` at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineHistory.test.ts:114-117`. The current `usePinnedGroupSync` coverage is in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts:644-976`. Step 4's proposed test run mentions `useTimelineHistory.test.ts`, `useTimelineCommit.test.tsx`, and `TimelineEditor.test.ts`, but not the hook test file that already exercises sync behavior.

flags.concern: Command-layer verification: the plan migrates both `updateToLatestVideo` and shot-drop pinned-group creation into the new command layer, but its explicit validation path does not cover either caller directly, and the repository currently has no automated test at all for `updateToLatestVideo`. That means the refactor can break the stale-final-video menu action while still satisfying the listed focused runs.
flags.evidence: `useSwitchToFinalVideo.updateToLatestVideo()` is implemented at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:254-297`, but `rg` finds no test coverage for that path in the current video-editor suites. The shot-drop pinned-group creation path being migrated in step 5 is covered today by `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx:537-590`, yet the plan's validation section only names `TimelineEditor`, `useTimelineCommit`, and `useClipEditing`, not `useExternalDrop`.
